Coding-Free Bioinformatics Summit 2026 Workshop Info Pre-work Schedule

Coding-Free Bioinformatics Summit 2026 Faculty: Ben Fisher, Larisa M. Soto, Nia Hughes, Kyster Nanan, Mohamed Helmy May 19, 2026 (online); May 22, 2026 (in-person) Workshop Info Welcome to the 2026 Coding-Free Bioinformatics Summit webpage! Participants of the Code-free Bioinformatics (CFB) Summit will work together to design an accessible entry point to bioinformatics for clinician scientists and health professionals in Canada. The resulting training course outline will be used to build a bioinformatics-skilled healthcare workforce. Empowering health professionals and clinical scientists with functional genomics literacy, knowledge of biases in genomics research and a deep understanding of patient experiences with precision medicine will ultimately facilitate the design and implementation standardized and interoperable data analysis frameworks, inclusive health policies surrounding genomic medicine, and equitable care for all Canadians. This is important because a bioinformatics-skilled healthcare workforce will not only drive implementation of genomics across the Canadian health research ecosystem; it will also build patient trust in genomics research by empowering healthcare professionals to communicate genomic-based diagnostics and therapeutics confidently and effectively. Meet Your Faculty Ben Fisher Regional Coordinator, CBH Atlantic Canadian Bioinformatics Hub, Dalhousie University Halifax, NS, Canada — atlantic@bioinformatics.ca Larisa Morales-Soto Platform Manager Canadian Bioinformatics Hub Toronto, ON, Canada — pm@bioinformatics.ca Nia Highes Training Manager Canadian Bioinformatics Hub Toronto, ON, Canada — training@bioinformatics.ca Kyster Nanan Education Lead & Project Manager, Ontario Molecular Pathology Research Network Queen’s University Kingston, ON, Canada — kn20@queensu.ca Mohamed Helmy Principal Scientist and Adjunct Professor, Vaccine and Infectious Disease Organization University of Saskatchewan Kingston, ON, Canada — mohamed.helmy@usask.ca
Day 1 (online) Welcome and Goals Case Presentation and Audience Definition Clinical Research Panel Patient Panel Learner Ecosystem Refinement

Day 1 (online) Welcome and Goals Participants will work together to act as architects of knowledge translation. As a whole, this group brings experience planning and facilitating education across Canada, designing innovative training modalities, and interfacing between experts and learners. Our goal is to combine our expertise and inform a case-based curriculum that makes advanced medical data accessible to audiences uninitiated to bioinformatics: this includes health professionals, researchers, and patients. We will ensure that national projects, resources and standards are included and effectively leveraged in the curriculum design. Together, we will bridge the gap between practical clinical application and the complexities of bioinformatics, computational biology and data science Case Presentation and Audience Definition Coming Soon.
